title: Agent Control Contract — A2A method set & core wire types
description: The A2A (Agent2Agent) JSON-RPC method set and the core object types a conformant agent serves over the substrate (vsock/unix) for an on-node gateway to bridge to HTTP/SSE. This is the method-registry companion to contract/v1/a2a.schema.json (the full type schema); it enumerates the served methods with both spellings and points params/result at the shared $defs. Extracted from agent RFC 0020 (the reference binding) + the A2A specification (https://a2a-protocol.org); reference source crates/agent/src/mcp/a2a.rs (dispatch_a2a) and crates/agent/src/capabilities.rs (a2a_surface). Per P0 the contract depends on this shape, never on a specific binary; agent is the reference impl. The agent serves ONLY the live task core (SendMessage/GetTask/CancelTask/ListTasks + the streaming pair); the gateway owns HTTP, SSE, OAuth/OIDC/mTLS, webhooks, durable history, version negotiation, and the push-notification-config + getAuthenticatedExtendedCard methods (agentctl RFC 0013). P2 BINDING-CONFORMANCE QUESTION (OPEN, agentctl RFC 0018 a2a.methods.json PENDING; brainstorm §14 P2): the reference impl dispatches PascalCase 'a2a.<Method>' names inside the NDJSON JSON-RPC envelope (RFC 0004) — this matches NEITHER the A2A JSON-RPC slash-form binding (message/send, tasks/get, ...) NOR the A2A gRPC/protobuf binding; it is a PascalCase-over-vsock convention. Committing to the slash-form is a real wire change, not a relabel. This file records BOTH spellings on every method: 'reference_method' (what is dispatched today, FROZEN for contract v1 as the reference binding) and 'spec_method' (the A2A-spec slash-form, the migration target). Which is normative is the unresolved binding-conformance decision; until commit the gateway is the translation layer.

protocol_version.description: A2A protocolVersion the agent advertises (surfaces.a2a.version, projected into the Agent Card). The reference impl pins '1.0' (capabilities.rs a2a_surface; mcp/a2a.rs module doc). PROVISIONAL: the gateway-served outward version may differ from the agent-advertised version (agentctl RFC 0013 §3.4); the gateway translates. Free string, not a const.

transport.description: Where the agent serves these methods. The reference impl serves the A2A surface over the SAME management listener (vsock/unix), gated to a PeerOrigin::Management peer (RFC 0015 §3.3); a Stdio peer gets -32601. P2 sub-question (OPEN): whether A2A gets its own surfaces.a2a.address or shares the management listener — the reference shares it and omits address.

methods.description: The served method set. Each entry records the reference spelling (FROZEN v1 reference binding, what dispatch_a2a routes today), the A2A-spec slash-form (migration target), and $refs into $defs for params/result. served_by: 'live' = the agent serves it; 'gateway' = the agent returns -32601 and the gateway owns it. The agent serves ONLY the six 'live' methods (mcp/a2a.rs dispatch_a2a; capabilities.rs a2a_surface methods[]).

error_codes.description: The JSON-RPC error codes the A2A surface returns (RFC 0020 / A2A spec; mcp/a2a.rs). Closed set.

TASK_NOT_FOUND.description: GetTask/CancelTask/SubscribeToTask for an id not in the live registry (mcp/a2a.rs TASK_NOT_FOUND).

METHOD_NOT_FOUND.description: Unsupported a2a method, or a Stdio-origin peer, or a gateway-owned method the agent does not serve.

INVALID_PARAMS.description: e.g. SendMessage with no non-empty text part.

MethodEntry.description: One A2A method, recorded in BOTH spellings (P2 binding-conformance OPEN). reference_method is the FROZEN v1 reference binding (PascalCase-over-vsock); spec_method is the A2A slash-form JSON-RPC binding (the migration target). params/result $ref the shared types.

reference_method.description: EXACT reference spelling dispatched today (mcp/a2a.rs dispatch_a2a; the a2a.<PascalCase> dotted name over the NDJSON JSON-RPC codec). Also the A2A gRPC/protobuf service-method name. The gateway MUST emit exactly this when bridging to the reference impl.

spec_method.description: A2A-spec slash-form JSON-RPC method (since 0.2.x) — the migration target. P2: not yet committed as normative.

served_by.description: live = the agent serves it (the live task core); gateway = the agent returns -32601, the gateway owns it (HTTP/SSE/push/durable history).

params.description: $ref into $defs for the request params type (omitted for no-param/gateway-owned methods).

result.description: $ref into $defs for the result type (omitted for gateway-owned methods).

streaming.description: true ⇒ the result is a same-id StreamResponse frame stream terminated by statusUpdate.final==true, NOT a unary reply (see StreamResponse).

x-entries.note: configuration.returnImmediately defaults true ⇒ async WORKING Task to poll; false ⇒ block to terminal Task (mcp/a2a.rs send_message).
Unknown id ⇒ -32001. Completed run carries the distillate artifact (distillate-only invariant, RFC 0009 §8).
Live run ⇒ CANCELED; already-terminal ⇒ its real terminal state (cancel-of-finished is a read). Unknown id ⇒ -32001.
LIVE instance-local registry only; durable cross-pod history is gateway-held (RFC 0020 §5/§7). Pagination params accepted; nextPageToken omitted (registry bounded by MAX_SESSIONS).

Status-level streaming: writes statusUpdate{WORKING,final:false}, then (on completion) artifactUpdate{distillate,lastChunk:true}, then RETURNS statusUpdate{<terminal>,final:true}. Same-id frames; read as a stream until final==true.
Resubscribe to an existing run by id. Unknown ⇒ -32001 (nothing streamed). Already-terminal ⇒ artifact (completed-only) + final frame now; live ⇒ WORKING then poll to terminal.
Gateway-owned; agent ⇒ -32601. The base card is the capabilities manifest (RFC 0015 §5.2).

TaskState.description: A2A TaskState, recorded in BOTH spellings (P2 OPEN, defeats naive codegen — needs a translation layer). reference_state is the proto-enum string the reference wire actually carries in status.state (mcp/a2a.rs TaskState::as_str); a2a_state is the A2A JSON-RPC lowercase binding. The four terminal states are completed/failed/canceled/rejected; submitted/working are in-flight; input-required/auth-required/unknown(unspecified) are non-terminal interaction/unknown states the reference agent never produces but a richer gateway peer might.

reference_state.description: FROZEN proto-enum strings (a2a.proto), the wire form the reference emits.

x-terminal-status-mapping.description: agent TerminalStatus (RFC 0007 §3.4) → A2A TaskState (mcp/a2a.rs terminal_to_state). Closed mapping. Synthetic 'running' → working; unknown → failed (never silently working).

Role.description: Message role, recorded in BOTH spellings (P2 OPEN; gateway-translated). The reference emits/consumes the proto spelling ROLE_USER (send_message_params); the A2A JSON-RPC binding is user/agent.

Part.description: An A2A message/artifact Part. The reference produces/consumes only the {text} variant (instruction_from_message reads only text parts; artifacts carry one text Part); other variants are tolerated (additive). Open object.

Message.description: An A2A Message. parts is required (≥1). The reference concatenates the text parts in order into the run instruction (mcp/a2a.rs instruction_from_message); a SendMessage with no non-empty text part ⇒ -32602. contextId/taskId support multi-turn/steering (P-attach-gate: a no-puppeting tenant may have this omitted at the manifest level).

Artifact.description: An A2A Artifact. parts is required (≥1). On a COMPLETED task the reference attaches EXACTLY ONE artifact — the distillate — with artifactId '<taskId>.distillate' and one text Part (mcp/a2a.rs task_object / artifact_update_frame; the distillate-only invariant, RFC 0009 §8). No partial-artifact streaming.

TaskStatus.description: An A2A TaskStatus: { state, timestamp? }. state carries a TaskState wire string (reference: a TASK_STATE_* proto string). timestamp is RFC 3339 millis, minted now (the agent is stateless; durable per-transition history is gateway-held).

state.description: A TaskState wire string — reference TASK_STATE_* (TaskState.reference_state) or, post-binding, an a2a_state lowercase string.

Task.description: An A2A Task (RFC 0020 §5). A served run IS a Task; id is the served handle (e.g. served.N). artifacts present ONLY on a terminal COMPLETED task (distillate-only invariant) — absent on working/failed/canceled/rejected. contextId is derived deterministically from the handle ('ctx-<handle>'). history is gateway-held (the agent is stateless).

artifacts.description: Present only on COMPLETED; the reference emits exactly one (the distillate).

TaskStatusUpdateEvent.description: The statusUpdate arm of a StreamResponse (A2A TaskStatusUpdateEvent). statusUpdate.final is the wire 'final' flag (mcp/a2a.rs status_update_frame, renamed from the Rust keyword): the gateway closes the SSE stream on the frame whose final==true. The agent emits a WORKING{final:false} opener and a terminal{final:true} closer.

final.description: Stream-termination flag. true ⇒ the last frame for this request id; the gateway closes the SSE stream. Consumers MUST read same-id frames as a stream until final==true, NOT as one unary reply.

TaskArtifactUpdateEvent.description: The artifactUpdate arm of a StreamResponse (A2A TaskArtifactUpdateEvent). Carries the distillate as ONE Artifact with one text Part; lastChunk:true marks the whole artifact (the agent never chunks — distillate-only, RFC 0009 §8). Emitted ONLY for a completed run (mcp/a2a.rs artifact_update_frame).

StreamResponse.description: The A2A StreamResponse oneof: { task | message | statusUpdate | artifactUpdate }. Carried as the 'result' of a same-id JSON-RPC reply frame: for one streaming request id the agent emits SEVERAL frames {jsonrpc,id,result:<StreamResponse>} (mcp/a2a.rs module doc — the agent↔gateway streaming convention). The agent emits ONLY the statusUpdate and artifactUpdate variants (status-level streaming). CONSUMER CONTRACT: read same-id frames as a STREAM until statusUpdate.final==true; do NOT treat them as duplicate unary replies. P-seq (OPEN): no monotonic per-frame seq, so gap-free SSE resume (Last-Event-ID) is not guaranteed — the gateway falls back to terminal-state reconstruction (agentctl RFC 0013 §3.3).

SendMessageParams.description: Params for SendMessage / SendStreamingMessage (A2A SendMessageRequest). configuration.returnImmediately defaults true (async WORKING Task to poll); false blocks to a terminal Task (mcp/a2a.rs send_message). For SendStreamingMessage the run is executed synchronously while the lifecycle is streamed.

GetTaskParams.description: Params for GetTask: { id, historyLength? } (mcp/a2a.rs task_id_param reads id). Unknown id ⇒ -32001.

TaskIdParams.description: Params for CancelTask / SubscribeToTask: { id } (mcp/a2a.rs task_id_param). Unknown id ⇒ -32001.

ListTasksParams.description: Params for ListTasks: { pageSize?, pageToken?, filter? }. Accepted but the ephemeral live registry returns the whole bounded set; nextPageToken omitted (mcp/a2a.rs list_tasks).

ListTasksResult.description: Result for ListTasks: { tasks }. LIVE instance-local registry only (mcp/a2a.rs list_tasks). nextPageToken omitted by the reference.
